top_stories.title: Microsoft makes passkeys default and retires SMS MFA by 2027
top_stories.summary: Microsoft is making passkeys the default authentication method in Microsoft Entra ID starting September 1, and will retire Microsoft-provided SMS and voice MFA in February 2027. Organizations relying on telephony-based MFA will need to migrate to alternative methods before the February 2027 deadline.

top_stories.title: Device Code Phishing: Turning a Convenience Feature Into an MFA Bypass
top_stories.summary: The article analyzes device code phishing, a technique that abuses OAuth 2.0 device authorization flow to bypass MFA by exploiting a legitimate authentication mechanism. It covers how the attack works, a recent observed case, and recommended defensive measures for organizations.

top_stories.title: Product Showcase: AppViewX Agent Identity Security
top_stories.summary: AppViewX has launched an Agent Identity Security product targeting the security of AI agent identities in enterprise environments. The solution addresses challenges of managing short-lived, autonomous AI agent credentials at scale, with Gartner projecting Fortune 500 enterprises will deploy over 150,000 AI agents by 2028.

top_stories.title: Police Dismantle Kratos Phishing Kit Built to Steal Microsoft 365 Sessions and Bypass MFA
top_stories.summary: German and US law enforcement dismantled the infrastructure of the Kratos phishing kit, which was designed to steal Microsoft 365 session tokens and bypass MFA. Indonesian authorities arrested the alleged developer and operator of the kit, which German investigators described as one of the most widely used criminal phishing tools globally.

top_stories.title: Hybrid identity turns into technical debt for cloud-only Microsoft environments
top_stories.summary: Hybrid identity configurations involving on-premises Active Directory and Entra Connect sync tools are creating technical debt in organizations running cloud-only Microsoft environments. The conflict arises as legacy AD domain controllers and synchronization infrastructure clash with modern cloud workloads in Microsoft Entra ID, Microsoft 365, and Intune deployments.

news.title: How enterprise GenAI can amplify ransomware risk — and how to contain it
news.summary: Acronis published guidance on how enterprise GenAI systems can amplify ransomware risk when AI assistants and agents inherit excessive permissions or compromised identities. The piece outlines how identity controls, governance, and least-privilege access principles apply to securing AI deployments against ransomware threats.

news.title: Chick-fil-A discloses data breach after credential stuffing attacks
news.summary: Chick-fil-A disclosed a data breach resulting from credential stuffing attacks that compromised customer accounts. The incident involved attackers using previously stolen credentials to gain unauthorized access to customer accounts on the chain's platform.

news.title: ThreatDown expands security visibility to AI tools and machine identities
news.summary: ThreatDown has launched AI visibility features and expanded its ITDR capabilities to cover non-human identities (NHI), including service accounts, API tokens, OAuth credentials, and machine identities. The product update targets MSPs and security teams seeking governance over unmanaged identity-related exposure points in their environments.

news.title: Police dismantle Kratos phishing platform, arrest developer
news.summary: German and U.S. authorities dismantled the Kratos phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform and arrested its developer in Indonesia. Kratos was a globally operating platform that enabled credential phishing attacks, which directly undermines authentication and identity security.

news.title: Qilin Ransomware Attackers Exploit PAN-OS Authentication Bypass for Initial Access
news.summary: Threat actors exploited a high-severity Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-0257, CVSS 7.8) as an initial access vector to deploy Qilin ransomware. Arctic Wolf Labs investigated multiple intrusions in June 2026 where attackers leveraged the flaw affecting PAN-OS portal and gateway components.

news.title: Critical ServiceNow AI Platform Flaw Exploited for Unauthenticated Code Execution
news.summary: A critical sandbox escape vulnerability (CVE-2026-6875, CVSS 9.5) in the ServiceNow AI Platform is being actively exploited in the wild, allowing unauthenticated users to execute arbitrary code. ServiceNow is widely used in enterprise environments for IT service management and integrates with IAM workflows, making unauthenticated remote code execution a direct concern for access control and identity infrastructure.

news.title: Disable SSO permission prompts in Windows 11
news.summary: Microsoft introduced a new registry policy in Windows 11 that allows administrators to automatically approve SSO permission prompts on managed devices. The change was driven by European Economic Area (EEA) regulatory requirements that had introduced mandatory user-facing SSO permission prompts.

news.title: Closing the Identity Gaps in Critical Infrastructure Security
news.summary: Specops Software published guidance on applying Zero Trust principles to critical infrastructure security. The article focuses on verifying both user identities and device trust before granting access, addressing attack vectors involving stolen credentials, compromised devices, and trusted accounts.

news.title: Teleport Establishes Agent Trust with New Identity Security Capabilities
news.summary: Teleport has launched new identity security capabilities focused on agent trust, including Session Summaries, Agentic Classifiers, and Risk Scoring features. These tools are designed to identify and prevent agent misalignment in agentic AI environments, extending Teleport's access security platform into AI/machine identity use cases.

news.title: Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk Services Platform
news.summary: A vulnerability in Rockwell Automation's FactoryTalk Services Platform (FTSP) allows attackers to impersonate authorized users on the FTSP server. Successful exploitation results in unauthorized access to system configurations, affecting specific versions of the platform.

news.title: Securing ASP.NET Core APIs with Microsoft Entra ID Authentication
news.summary: The article covers how to secure ASP.NET Core APIs using Microsoft Entra ID authentication. It addresses authentication configuration and best practices for API security using Microsoft's IAM platform.

news.title: HOLLOWGRAPH malware turns Microsoft 365 calendars into an espionage channel
news.summary: Group-IB researchers discovered HOLLOWGRAPH malware that uses Microsoft 365 calendar appointments dated 2050 to hide commands and exfiltrate stolen data. The campaign has been tied to Iranian espionage activity targeting Microsoft 365 environments.

news.title: Security Is a Platform Property, Not a Pipeline Step
news.summary: The article describes a practitioner's experience disabling key-based authentication on an Azure storage account in favor of RBAC-only permissions and Privileged Identity Management (PIM) for Terraform state management. The account covers the implementation of Azure RBAC, PIM approval workflows, and the broader principle of treating security controls—including identity and access management—as platform-level properties rather than pipeline steps.

news.title: authID Selected by Top Global Retailer to Deploy Biometric Authentication for 100K+ Employees
news.summary: authID has been selected by a major global retailer to deploy biometric authentication for over 100,000 employees. The implementation will cover employee onboarding and access management, targeting prevention of ransomware and other attack vectors.

news.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
news.summary: Identity-based attacks have surpassed exploits as the leading root cause of ransomware incidents. MFA was present in 97% of credential-based attacks yet failed to prevent compromise, highlighting critical gaps in authentication defenses.

news.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
news.summary: CISA released a postmortem on a data breach in which a contractor accidentally published dozens of internal CISA credentials, including AWS GovCloud keys, to a public GitHub repository for nearly six months. The incident highlights credential management failures and secrets exposure risks relevant to IAM and PAM practices.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to a 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise enterprise systems.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to bypass account security and reset Instagram passwords, briefly compromising high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force Chief Master Sergeant. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to perform unauthorized password resets were circulated on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.
